title: Imprecise citation of BGLS 'sum of top-k hyperbolic eigenvalues is convex'; hyperbolicity of f not verified

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Two issues around Theorem (thm:bgls)/Corollary (cor:psd):

1. The statement quoted — '$\sigma_k(a)=\sum_{i\le k}\lambda_i(a)$ is convex' — is the hyperbolic Ky-Fan theorem. Bauschke–Güler–Lewis–Sendov's paper centers on convexity of the largest eigenvalue and of the hyperbolicity cone; the 'sum of top $k$' extension is true but is a derived statement. As written the reader cannot confirm the exact form invoked is the one proved in [BGLS]. Either cite the precise result/location, or prove the needed instance (e.g. via the variational representation $\sigma_k=\max_{\dim S=k}\sum_{i\in S}\lambda_i$).

2. Hyperbolicity of $f(x,a,b)=\frac1{n!}\sum_{\pi}\prod_i(x-a_i-b_{\pi(i)})$ in direction $e_1$ is asserted 'by Walsh's theorem' with no argument. The justification is: for every real $(a,b)$, $t\mapsto f(x+t,a,b)=(p\boxplus q)(x+t)$ is real-rooted (Walsh), hence $f$ is hyperbolic in $e_1$ on all of $\mathbb R^{2n+1}$. This should be written out — it is the single place real-rootedness of $\boxplus$ enters essentially.
